description: REQ-FEED-024 EARS clause describes the emit payload as a flat object `{ status: "editing", isDirty: false, currentNoteId: note_id, pendingNextNoteId: null, lastError: null, body: <note body from file> }`, but the actual wire format produced by `editor::make_editing_state_changed_payload` at editor.rs:161-178 wraps this inside `{ state: EditingSessionStateDto }`. `editorStateChannel.ts:29` then extracts `event.payload.state`. A developer reading only REQ-FEED-024 would emit a flat object without the `{ state: ... }` wrapper, causing EditorPane to receive `undefined` at `event.payload.state` and fail silently.

evidence.snippet: THEN the system SHALL ALSO emit `editing_session_state_changed` with `{ status: "editing", isDirty: false, currentNoteId: note_id, pendingNextNoteId: null, lastError: null, body: <note body from file> }`.

remediationSuggestion: Either (a) add a cross-reference to REQ-EDIT-036 clarifying that `editing_session_state_changed` payload conforms to the `{ state: EditingSessionStateDto }` wrapper contract, or (b) describe the full payload shape including the `{ state: ... }` wrapping in the EARS clause. The traceability table at line 429 already references `editor.rs make_editing_state_changed_payload` but this is not sufficient — the EARS clause itself must be accurate.
